a: A way to organize and store data.
exp: Like special organizers (cubbies, stacks, files) so the computer can find things instantly.
Time checks how many steps an algorithm takes. Space checks how much memory it uses.
Like a numbered pill box. Finding an item by index is instant O(1)!
To insert in the middle, you have to push everyone else to the right. Very slow!
Data isn't stored together. Node A points to Node B. Node B points to Node C.
Singly: Next pointer only. Doubly: Next AND Prev pointers.
Last node links back to the Head instead of pointing to NULL.
Last In, First Out. Like a stack of pancakes. You eat the top one first.
Push puts an item on top, Pop removes the item from the top.
First In, First Out. Like a line at the supermarket.
Enqueue joins the back. Dequeue leaves the front.
Math trick using Modulo to make the array wrap around like a circle, saving empty front slots.
Has a Root at top, branches, and Leaves (nodes with no children) at bottom.
A node can have 0, 1, or 2 children, but NEVER 3.
Left child is always smaller than the root, Right child is always bigger.
Prints the BST perfectly in ascending sorted order!
Visits the parent before its children.
Visits the children entirely before the parent.
The balance factor (left height - right height) MUST be -1, 0, or 1. If not, it rotates.
The twists used to unkink the tree if it gets out of balance.
Checking every box one by one from 0 to N. Works on unsorted arrays.
Checking the middle and cutting the dictionary in half. The array MUST be sorted first.
Dots (Vertices) connected by lines (Edges). Like airplane route maps.
Directed has arrows on edges. Undirected has plain lines.
Every dot is connected directly to every other dot.
Graph storage where grid says 1 if connected, 0 if not.
Saves memory if the graph is sparse (few edges).
Turning a name directly into a locker number. Extremely fast O(1) searches.
When two items math out to the exact same locker.
Collision? Walk one step forward until you find a free spot.
Collision? Check +1, +4, +9 slots away to avoid clustering.
Collision? Make a little Linked List right there in the locker.
Push 5, Push 3, + pops them, adds them, pushes 8.

q: Infix to Postfix: A + B * C
exp: Multiplication has priority.
Pops 2, then 6. calculates 6 / 2 = 3.
Last In First Out (Like undo in Word).
First In First Out (Like a line at a store).

q: What is the worst case time for Binary Search?
exp: Data halves each step.

q: What is required before using Binary Search?
exp: You can't eliminate halves if data is scrambled.
Probing and Chaining solve collisions.

q: A node with no children in a tree is a...
exp: Leaves are at the bottom.

exp: Math formula for complete undirected graph.

exp: Math calculation of exact memory offset.

q: In a Doubly Linked List, nodes have pointers for:
exp: Allows two-way traffic.

q: What traversal processes Root, Left, Right?
exp: Pre means the root comes BEFORE the children.

q: What does a balanced AVL tree forbid?
exp: If |left - right| is 2, it rotates.

q: How do you insert an existing key in a basic BST?
exp: BSTs typically hold unique values.

q: Which structure best models a maze solver Backtracking?
exp: Remembers exactly how you came in to retrace steps.
